close_reason: Already documented in module-level docs (lines 33-37). The non-atomic behavior is inherent to wolfHSM NVM API and documented with the exact failure mode. Adding the same text to the method doc is a polish item, not a bug.
description: WolfHsmBackend in secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs implements WritableSecretStore::put() as a delete-then-add sequence (non-atomic). If the process is killed between the delete and the add, the secret is permanently lost. This is documented in a comment inside the put() implementation body and in the module-level docs, but not in the put() method's doc comment where a caller inspecting the WritableSecretStore impl via rustdoc would look. The WritableSecretStore trait itself does not require atomicity, so this is a valid implementation — but callers need to know about the durability risk. What goes wrong: an operator rotates a credential via put() on a WolfHSM, the process is killed mid-operation, and the secret slot is empty with no way to recover. Fix: add a /// doc comment to WolfHsmBackend's put() implementation (using #[doc] override or adding a method-level doc) warning that put() is not atomic on this backend and data loss is possible if interrupted.
title: WolfHsmBackend::put() non-atomic delete+add undocumented at trait level
